sections.paragraph: twice כִּסֵּה Job 26:9 1Ki 10:19 with suff. כִּסְאִי (for כִּסְּאִי); pl. כִּסְאֹות (for כִּסְּאֹות) m. a seat, lofty and covered with a canopy or hanging, (from the root כָּסָא, כָּסָה); hence, a royal throne, Job 36:7 2Sa 3:10 fully כּ׳ הַמַּמְלָכָה 2Sa 7:13 כּ׳ הַמַּלְכוּת 1Ch 22:10 2Ch 7:18 also that of God, Jer 3:17 the tribunal of a judge, Psa 122:5 Neh 3:7 the seat of the high priest, 1Sa 1:9 4:13 rarely used of a common seat, 2Ki 4:10 Pro 9:14. (Arab. كُرِْسُّ, Aram. ܟܘܽܪܣܝܐܳ, כָּרְסֵא id., the letter רֽ being inserted before the letter which had been doubled, rs for ss; see below under the letter . In the root itself the Samaritans have for כִּסָּה, Ecc 6:1-12
